scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the real, current Pennylane API v2 reference (159 documented endpoints, retrieved via https://pennylane.readme.io/llms.txt and individual reference pages' embedded OpenAPI specs). 5 read streams + 8 write actions pre-existed from wave2; Pass B adds 3 read streams (supplier_invoices, transactions, bank_accounts), 1 write action (create_product), and stop_path:has_more to the shared cursor pagination (the docs describe has_more, not next_cursor absence alone, as the authoritative stop signal). Every remaining endpoint is excluded with a specific reason: duplicate_of covers single-resource-by-id GETs and per-parent sub-resource lists this bundle's existing/new list streams already return in full or has no id source to fan out over; requires_elevated_scope covers real capability gaps (compound multi-line-item writes, lifecycle/status-transition writes, reconciliation/matching writes, and entire resource domains â€” ledger/journals under the documented 2026 scope migration, billing_subscriptions, quotes, purchase_requests, mandates â€” not yet modeled) tracked for future increments, never silently faked; binary_payload covers every multipart file-upload endpoint (this dialect has no multipart body_type); non_data_endpoint covers async export jobs, computed reports, and the /me endpoint; out_of_scope covers pure notification/email-send side effects with no reverse-ETL analog; deprecated covers the one endpoint the docs themselves mark deprecated.

excluded.reason: kicks off an async Analytical General Ledger export job (returns a job id polled by a separate GET); no declarative StreamHook-free way to poll an async job to completion and no natural stream/write shape for a fire-and-poll accounting export

retrieves a single async export job's status/result by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source (ids only come from the POST above, which this bundle does not call) and no meaningful stream shape for a one-off job poll

kicks off an async FEC export job (returns a job id polled by a separate GET); no declarative StreamHook-free way to poll an async job to completion and no natural stream/write shape for a fire-and-poll accounting export

retrieves a single async export job's status/result by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source (ids only come from the POST above, which this bundle does not call) and no meaningful stream shape for a one-off job poll

kicks off an async General Ledger export job (returns a job id polled by a separate GET); no declarative StreamHook-free way to poll an async job to completion and no natural stream/write shape for a fire-and-poll accounting export

retrieves a single async export job's status/result by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source (ids only come from the POST above, which this bundle does not call) and no meaningful stream shape for a one-off job poll

a small, rarely-changing per-company configuration list (fiscal year boundaries) rather than an operational data stream; genuinely low-value as a sync stream but not modeled today, tracked for a future increment rather than silently promoted to a stream
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the journals list covers the same fields

a ledger-scoped accounting configuration list undergoing a documented 2026 API behavior migration (old ledger scope vs new journals:readonly/all scopes); deferred until the migration's sunset phase completes rather than committing to a shape that changes under this bundle
creates a new accounting journal (ledger structure), not a transactional data write; same ledger-scope migration caveat as List journals above
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the ledger accounts list covers the same fields
chart-of-accounts configuration list under the same documented 2026 ledger-scope migration as journals above; deferred until the migration completes
creates a chart-of-accounts ledger account, an accounting configuration write rather than a transactional data write; deferred alongside the ledger-scope migration
updates a chart-of-accounts ledger account; same accounting-configuration deferral as create above
docs mark this endpoint DEPRECATED in favor of the File Attachments Upload endpoint (postfileattachments); not modeled per the deprecated-endpoint convention
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the ledger entries list covers the same fields

raw double-entry bookkeeping journal-entry list undergoing the same documented 2026 ledger-scope migration as journals/ledger_accounts above; deferred until the migration completes rather than committing to a pre-migration shape
per-parent sub-resource list with no dynamic ledger-entry-id enumeration source given ledger_entries itself is deferred above
creates a raw double-entry bookkeeping journal entry; deferred alongside the ledger-scope migration
updates a raw ledger entry; same deferral
un-letters (unreconciles) ledger entry lines; an accounting-reconciliation write deferred alongside the ledger-scope migration
single-resource fetch by id; deferred alongside ledger_entries above
raw ledger-entry-line list; deferred alongside the ledger-scope migration
per-parent sub-resource list; deferred alongside ledger_entry_lines above
per-parent sub-resource list; deferred alongside ledger_entry_lines above
reconciles (letters) ledger entry lines together; deferred alongside the ledger-scope migration
replaces analytical categories on a ledger entry line; deferred alongside the ledger-scope migration
returns a computed accounting report (trial balance for a period), not individual records with a stable primary key across syncs
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the categories list stream already returns every field this endpoint does
per-parent sub-resource view of records the categories stream already returns in full (every category carries its own category_group_id)
per-id fetch of a small, rarely-changing grouping-configuration resource with no bundle-level id source to enumerate; tracked for a future fan_out increment alongside List category groups
a small, rarely-changing grouping-configuration list (category groups organize categories) rather than an operational data stream; not modeled today, tracked for a future increment
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the billing subscriptions list would cover the same fields if modeled
per-parent sub-resource list with no bundle-level billing-subscription-id source to fan out over
per-parent sub-resource list; same gap as invoice_lines above

Pennylane's own recurring-billing-subscription feature (customers billed by Pennylane itself), a distinct resource from the customer_invoices/quotes this bundle already reads; not modeled today, tracked for a future increment

creates a Pennylane billing subscription that starts generating recurring customer invoices automatically; a real capability gap tracked for a future increment given the List endpoint above is not yet modeled either
updates a Pennylane billing subscription; same deferral as create above

a change-feed (create/update/delete diff events, retained only 4 weeks) over records the customers stream already reads in full via ordinary polling; the customers stream is the source of truth this bundle syncs, not the ephemeral changelog

per-parent sub-resource list with no bundle-level commercial-document-id source to fan out over
per-parent sub-resource list; same gap as appendices above
per-parent sub-resource list; same gap as appendices above

a unified read-only view spanning customer invoices, credit notes, and quotes this bundle already reads via its own dedicated customer_invoices stream (and quotes, if added) — modeling it as a second stream would duplicate the same underlying records under a different envelope
multipart file upload (an appendix attachment); this dialect's declarative body_type is json/form/none, no multipart support
a small, rarely-changing invoice-template configuration list rather than an operational data stream; not modeled today
imports a customer invoice from an uploaded e-invoice file (Factur-X PDF / UBL / CII XML); requires a binary file body, no multipart support in this dialect
creates a customer invoice from an existing quote; requires the quotes resource (not modeled — see quotes below) as its id source

deletes a single draft invoice by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source distinct from the customer_invoices stream's own ids, and a delete-by-id write action needs a real record with that id supplied by the caller rather than an enumerable id list — tracked as a future write-action increment, not a stream gap
converts a draft invoice/credit note to finalized (irreversible, no further edits); a lifecycle-transition write distinct from ordinary field updates, tracked for a future increment
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the customer_invoices stream already returns every field this endpoint does
per-parent sub-resource list with no bundle-level customer-invoice-id source to fan out over economically
per-parent sub-resource list; same gap as appendices above

per-parent sub-resource list; same gap as appendices above
imports an invoice with an attached file; requires a binary file body, no multipart support in this dialect
links an existing credit note to a customer invoice; a relationship-linking write distinct from ordinary field updates, tracked for a future increment
marks an invoice paid without automatic bank reconciliation; a lifecycle/status-transition write distinct from ordinary field updates, tracked for a future increment

creates a draft or finalized customer invoice/credit note with nested invoice-line items; a materially more complex compound write (line items, tax computation, category links) than this bundle's existing single-object create/update actions, tracked for a future increment rather than a partial/lossy model
per-invoice analytical-category assignment; deferred alongside the customer invoice create/update actions above
triggers Pennylane's own transactional email delivery of an invoice PDF to the customer; a notification side-effect, not a data mutation with a reverse-ETL analog

submits a customer e-invoice to the French Plateforme Agréée (PA) e-invoicing network; a regulatory-submission action with its own irreversible state machine, tracked for a future increment rather than modeled as an ordinary write
updates a customer invoice's nested line items/categories; deferred alongside the create action above given the same compound-write complexity
updates an imported (non-draft) customer invoice; same compound-write deferral as ordinary update above
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the customers stream already returns every field this endpoint does
single-resource fetch by id; no dynamic-id enumeration source and the customers stream already returns every field this endpoint does

multipart file upload (max 100MB) producing an attachment id consumed by other import/appendix endpoints; this dialect's declarative body_type is json/form/none, no multipart support

deletes a customer's SEPA direct-debit mandate; a payment-instrument write with no bundle-level mandate-id source to target, tracked for a future increment alongside the mandate list/create actions below

multipart file upload (a quote appendix); no multipart support in this dialect
creates a quote with nested line items; deferred alongside the quotes list given quotes are not modeled as a stream/resource in this bundle yet

creates a manual banking transaction; a distinct financial-record write from the customer/supplier/product mutations this bundle exposes today, tracked for a future increment
